title: eranthemum hispidum
intro: Eranthemum hispidum, also known as the hairy eranthemum or hairy zinnia, is a flowering plant species native to the Western Ghats of India. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, Eranthemum hispidum is often used in the form of decoctions or infusions, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to treat skin conditions.
